# Link Grid

The link grid component displays a collection of links in a responsive multi-column grid layout. It provides flexible theming and visual treatment options for organizing groups of related links with clear visual separation.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the link grid's properties in real-time.

<Canvas of={LinkGridStories.linkGrid} />
<Controls of={LinkGridStories.linkGrid} />

## Properties

### Optional Properties

{twigPropsTable(componentProps, 'optional')}

## Theme Options

This component supports the following theme variations:

- **One through Five**: Custom theme dials that override global settings with component-specific colors

Use the `link_grid__theme` property to apply different themes. The theme affects link colors, hover states, and visual accents.

## Line Treatment Options

The link grid offers four line treatment options for visual separation:

- **default**: Standard line treatment with contextual styling
- **all_strong_lines**: Bold, prominent dividing lines between all items
- **all_light_lines**: Subtle, light dividing lines between all items
- **no_lines**: No dividing lines, clean spacing only

Choose the line treatment that best fits your content density and visual hierarchy needs.

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-link-grid.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-link-grid.scss`

### Accessibility

- Uses semantic HTML list structure for proper screen reader navigation
- All links are keyboard accessible with clear focus indicators
- Screen readers announce the grid structure and total link count
- Color themes meet WCAG contrast requirements

### Behavior

- Responsive grid layout adapts from multi-column to single column on mobile
- Standard link styling with theme-aware hover and focus states
- Grid items automatically reflow based on container width
- Works without JavaScript

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use link grids for collections of 6-20 related links
- Choose line treatments that complement the surrounding content density
- Select themes that provide sufficient contrast with the page background
- Ensure link text is concise and descriptive (2-5 words recommended)
- Group related links together for easier scanning

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use link grids for very small link sets (use link group instead)
- Avoid mixing link grids with vastly different line treatments on the same page
- Don't use overly long link text that disrupts the grid layout
- Avoid creating too many link grid sections that overwhelm the page

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all link grid variations, theme combinations, and line treatments, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-link-grid-visreg).

## Related Components

- **Link Group**: Use for simple vertical list of links without grid layout
- **Card Collection**: Alternative for presenting links with additional context and imagery
- **Menu**: Consider for primary navigation structures
